mnemonics: K-NAIVE: Knapsack Never Achieves Ideal Valuations
keyPoints: A knapsack can hold capacity W, and we have W items with weights w1, w2, ..., wn and values v1, v2, ..., vn
The K-NB approach uses a 2D table dp[i][j] to store the maximum value that can be obtained with the first i items and capacity j
The K-NAIVE approach calculates the maximum value by trying all possible subsets of items, which results in O(2^n * W) time complexity
